Output: sitemap (hierarchical page structure with every page in the app: page_name, url_path, parent_page, auth_required, description), content_grouping (how features are grouped into navigation sections), navigation_model (primary: top bar vs sidebar vs bottom tabs, secondary: breadcrumbs vs back button, utility: settings, profile, notifications), page_types (dashboard, list, detail, form, settings, empty, error — define what each type looks like structurally). The sitemap must cover every screen needed to fulfill all user stories in mvp_scope.
steps.done_when: information_architecture exists with complete sitemap, navigation model, and page type definitions.

steps.name: navigation_design
steps.instruction: Design the navigation system in detail. Specify: primary_navigation (items shown: label, icon_name, url, badge/count indicator, active state detection), secondary_navigation (per-section sub-navigation if needed), user_menu (profile, settings, billing, logout — where it lives, how it opens), notification_access (bell icon, unread count, dropdown vs page), search_access (search bar placement, always visible vs expandable), breadcrumb_rules (which pages show breadcrumbs, format), mobile_navigation (bottom tab bar items, hamburger menu contents, gesture navigation), keyboard_shortcuts (global shortcuts: Cmd+K for search, etc.), deep_linking (how direct URLs work for every page, URL structure for shareable content), back_behavior (browser back button expectations per page type), navigation_state_persistence (does selected tab survive page refresh? does scroll position persist?).
steps.done_when: navigation_design exists with complete navigation specification for all platforms and interaction modes.

steps.name: user_flow_auth
steps.instruction: Diagram the authentication user flows screen-by-screen. For each flow, list every screen in order with: screen_name, screen_purpose, elements_shown (form fields, buttons, links, messages), user_actions_available, transitions (action → next screen), error_states (what shows on validation failure, wrong password, etc.). Flows to document: signup_email (landing → signup form → email verification sent → verify link clicked → onboarding), signup_oauth (landing → OAuth provider → callback → account created → onboarding), login_email (login form → dashboard, or → error states), login_oauth (login → OAuth → dashboard), password_reset (forgot link → email form → email sent → reset link → new password form → success → login), logout (any page → confirmation if needed → landing). Include: what happens if user visits signup while already logged in, what happens if verification link expires.
steps.done_when: user_flow_auth exists with every auth flow diagrammed screen-by-screen with transitions and error states.

steps.name: user_flow_onboarding
steps.instruction: Design the onboarding flow that takes a new user from account creation to their first value moment (the 'aha moment' from user_journey_map). Screen-by-screen: screen_name, purpose, content (what the user sees), actions (what they do), skip_option (can they skip this step?), progress_indicator (step 2 of 4), data_collected (what we learn about the user at this step). The onboarding should: be completable in under 2 minutes, collect only information that immediately improves the experience, demonstrate the core value proposition, have a clear completion state. Also design: what happens if user abandons onboarding midway (resume on next login?), what happens if user skips entirely (progressive onboarding with tooltips?), re-onboarding triggers (new features, idle users returning).
steps.done_when: user_flow_onboarding exists with every screen, skip logic, abandonment handling, and progressive disclosure strategy.

steps.name: user_flow_core_features
steps.instruction: For each core feature in the MVP, diagram the complete user flow. For each flow: flow_name, entry_points (how the user gets to this flow: navigation, link, notification, URL), screens (ordered list, each with: screen_name, purpose, data_displayed, actions_available, form_fields if any), transitions (action → next screen, with conditions), success_state (what the user sees on completion), error_states (per screen: what errors can occur, how they are displayed, recovery actions), loading_states (per screen: what shows while data loads), empty_states (what shows when there's no data yet), confirmation_dialogs (destructive actions that need confirmation), undo_support (can the action be undone? how?). Cover create, read, update, delete flows for each core entity.
steps.done_when: user_flow_core_features exists with complete flows for every core feature including all states.

steps.name: user_flow_settings
steps.instruction: Diagram user flows for settings and account management. Flows: profile_editing (view profile → edit mode → save → confirmation), password_change (current password → new password → confirmation), email_change (new email → verification → confirmed), notification_preferences (toggle per notification type → auto-save), billing_management (view plan → upgrade/downgrade → payment → confirmation), payment_method_update (current method → add new → set default → remove old), account_deletion (request → confirmation dialog → cooldown period → final deletion, with data export option before deletion), connected_accounts (view OAuth connections → connect new → disconnect existing). For each flow include security re-authentication requirements (e.g., confirm password before email change).
steps.done_when: user_flow_settings exists with all settings and account management flows fully diagrammed.

steps.name: wireframe_layout_system
steps.instruction: Define the page layout system. Specify: app_shell (the persistent frame: header height, sidebar width, content area dimensions, footer if any), layout_templates (for each page type: dashboard_layout with grid/cards, list_layout with table/cards and filters/sort bar, detail_layout with header and content sections, form_layout with sections and action bar, settings_layout with sidebar navigation and content, auth_layout with centered card), grid_system (column count, gutter width, margin, max content width), spacing_scale (4px base: 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64, 96), responsive_breakpoints (mobile: 0-767px, tablet: 768-1023px, desktop: 1024-1439px, wide: 1440px+), per_breakpoint_layout_changes (what collapses, stacks, or hides at each breakpoint). Describe each layout template in enough detail for implementation.
steps.done_when: layout_system_spec exists with all layout templates, grid system, spacing, and responsive rules defined.

steps.name: wireframe_core_screens
steps.instruction: Create wireframe specifications for every unique screen in the MVP. For each screen: screen_id (SCR-001...), screen_name, url_path, layout_template (from layout_system_spec), content_zones (ordered top-to-bottom, left-to-right, each zone: zone_name, component_type, data_displayed, actions_available, position_description), element_hierarchy (heading levels, visual weight priority: what should the user see first, second, third), interactive_elements (every button, link, form field, toggle — with label and action), conditional_elements (what appears/disappears based on state: logged in, role, data presence, feature flag). Focus on content hierarchy and information architecture, not visual design. Use text descriptions, not visual layouts.
steps.done_when: wireframe_specs exists with every unique screen fully described in terms of content, hierarchy, and interactions.

steps.name: wireframe_forms
steps.instruction: Define detailed specifications for every form in the application. For each form: form_id, screen_id (where it lives), fields (ordered list, each field: field_name, label, input_type, placeholder, required, validation_rules from business_rules, error_message_per_rule, help_text, default_value, max_length, autocomplete_attribute), field_grouping (logical sections with headings), multi_step_config (if form is multi-step: steps, fields per step, progress indicator, back/next behavior, save draft?), submit_button (label, position, loading state label), cancel_behavior (confirm if unsaved changes), auto_save (if applicable: debounce interval, visual indicator), keyboard_behavior (tab order, enter to submit, escape to cancel).
steps.done_when: form_specs exists with every form field, validation rule, grouping, and interaction behavior defined.

steps.name: wireframe_empty_error_states
steps.instruction: Define all empty and error state screens. Empty states to design (for each: illustration_description, headline, body_text, cta_button): first_time_empty (user has no data yet — guide them to create first item), search_no_results (query returned nothing — suggest broadening search), filtered_no_results (filters are too restrictive — suggest clearing filters), list_empty (section has no items — explain what goes here and how to add), offline_state (no internet connection — show cached data if any, explain limitation). Error states: 404_not_found, 403_forbidden, 500_server_error, maintenance_mode, session_expired, rate_limited. For each error: headline, body_text, primary_action (button label and destination), secondary_action (if applicable), tone (helpful, not blaming).
steps.done_when: empty_error_state_specs exists with content and CTA for every empty and error state.

steps.name: wireframe_review
steps.instruction: Review all wireframe specifications against user stories and flows. Check: 1) Every user story's acceptance criteria can be fulfilled by the screens defined, 2) Every flow from user_flow_* has all its screens in wireframe_specs, 3) No dead ends (every screen has a way forward and a way back), 4) Consistent patterns (similar features use similar layouts), 5) All form fields match the data requirements and validation rules, 6) Empty and error states exist for every applicable screen, 7) Navigation is reachable from every screen, 8) No missing screens (check user_flow transitions — is every target screen defined?). Output: status, missing_screens, issues per screen, flow_gaps.
steps.done_when: wireframe_review_result exists identifying any gaps between flows, stories, and screen specifications.

steps.name: brand_identity
steps.instruction: Define the brand identity. Specify: brand_personality (3-5 adjective traits, e.g., 'professional yet approachable, modern, trustworthy'), tone_of_voice (how the product speaks: formal vs casual, technical vs simple, playful vs serious — with examples of each tone applied to common UI text), color_psychology (what emotions the colors should evoke given the product domain and audience), typography_personality (rounded vs geometric vs humanist — what feeling the type should convey), visual_style_direction (flat vs subtle depth, minimalist vs rich, illustration style if any). Base recommendations on: product_vision (what we aspire to be), user_personas (what resonates with them), competitor_ux_evaluation (how to differentiate visually). If the product domain allows multiple valid directions (e.g., playful vs professional), present options and trigger needs_clarification to ask human.
steps.done_when: brand_identity exists with personality, tone of voice, and visual direction defined.

steps.name: design_token_specification
steps.instruction: Define all design tokens as exact values. Color tokens: primary (50-950 shade scale), secondary, accent, neutral/gray (50-950), semantic (success_green, warning_amber, error_red, info_blue — each with default, light, dark variants), background (page, card, elevated, overlay), text (primary, secondary, tertiary, disabled, inverse), border (default, hover, focus). Typography tokens: font_family_heading, font_family_body, font_family_mono, size_scale (xs through 4xl with px values), weight_scale (regular 400, medium 500, semibold 600, bold 700), line_height_scale (tight, normal, relaxed), letter_spacing_scale. Spacing tokens: 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24 (in 4px base units). Border tokens: radius (none, sm, md, lg, xl, full), width (1px, 2px). Shadow tokens: sm, md, lg, xl. Transition tokens: duration (fast 150ms, normal 200ms, slow 300ms), easing (ease-in-out). Z-index tokens: dropdown 10, sticky 20, modal 30, popover 40, toast 50. All color values in hex. All sizes in px or rem.
steps.done_when: design_tokens exists with every token category fully specified with exact values.

steps.name: component_spec_primitives
steps.instruction: Specify every primitive UI component. For each component: component_name, description, variants (list with visual differences), sizes (sm, md, lg with exact dimensions), states (default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading — describe visual change for each state using design tokens), props (configurable properties with types and defaults), accessibility (ARIA role, keyboard interaction, focus behavior, screen reader announcement), responsive_behavior (any size/layout changes per breakpoint). Components to specify: Button (primary, secondary, ghost, danger, link variants), Input (text, email, password, number, search, with icon support), Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle/Switch, Label, HelperText, Badge, Avatar (image, initials, icon fallback), Icon (sizing convention, color inheritance), Divider, Spinner/Loader, Skeleton, Link, Tag/Chip.
steps.done_when: component_spec_primitives exists with full specification for every primitive component.

steps.name: component_spec_composite
steps.instruction: Specify every composite UI component. For each: component_name, composed_of (which primitives), variants, sizes, states, props, accessibility, responsive_behavior. Components to specify: Card (header, body, footer sections, clickable variant), Modal/Dialog (sizes, overlay, close mechanisms: X button + escape + overlay click, focus trap, scroll behavior, header/body/footer), Toast/Notification (variants: info/success/warning/error, auto-dismiss timing, stack behavior, position), Alert/Banner (inline, dismissible, with icon and action), Dropdown/Menu (trigger, items, dividers, icons, keyboard navigation), Tooltip (trigger: hover/focus, placement, delay), Popover (trigger: click, content, placement, close behavior), Tabs (horizontal/vertical, lazy loading, URL sync), Accordion (single/multi expand, animation), Table (sortable headers, row selection, pagination, responsive collapse behavior), Pagination (page numbers, prev/next, items per page selector), Breadcrumb, EmptyState (illustration slot, heading, body, CTA), Sidebar, Navbar, Footer.
steps.done_when: component_spec_composite exists with full specification for every composite component.

steps.name: screen_specifications
steps.instruction: For every screen in wireframe_specs, create a detailed screen specification that an implementer can code from. For each screen: screen_id, layout_template, component_tree (hierarchical: which components contain which, with exact props and token references), data_bindings (which data fields map to which component props), action_handlers (which component events trigger which actions: API calls, navigation, state changes), conditional_rendering (show/hide rules for each conditional element), loading_state (which components show skeleton, which show spinner, loading sequence), error_state (where errors display, which component shows them), responsive_overrides (what changes at each breakpoint: hidden elements, stacked layouts, component size changes). This is the definitive implementation reference for each screen.
steps.done_when: screen_specifications exists with implementation-ready specs for every screen using exact components and tokens.

steps.name: interaction_specification
steps.instruction: Define all micro-interactions and animations. Categories: transitions (page transitions: type and duration, modal open/close: scale + fade, sidebar collapse/expand, accordion expand/collapse, tab switch), feedback (button press: scale down slightly, form submission: button loading state, success: toast slide in from top-right, error: shake animation on invalid field), loading (skeleton shimmer animation, progress bar for uploads, spinner for short waits, skeleton for longer waits — threshold: show spinner after 200ms, switch to skeleton after 1s), scroll (infinite scroll trigger point, scroll-to-top button appearance threshold, sticky header behavior), drag (if applicable: drag handle cursor, drag ghost opacity, drop zone highlight). For each: css_property_animated, duration (from design_tokens), easing (from design_tokens), trigger_condition. Also specify: reduced_motion_behavior (what changes when prefers-reduced-motion is set).
steps.done_when: interaction_spec exists with every animation, transition, and feedback behavior defined with timing values.

steps.name: responsive_specification
steps.instruction: For each screen, define explicit responsive behavior at every breakpoint. For each screen × breakpoint combination: layout_changes (what shifts from row to column, what goes from sidebar to stacked), hidden_elements (what's hidden on mobile: secondary nav, decorative elements, advanced filters), navigation_changes (top bar → bottom tab bar, sidebar → hamburger menu), component_changes (table → card list on mobile, horizontal tabs → dropdown on mobile, multi-column → single column), touch_adaptations (larger touch targets on mobile, swipe gestures, pull-to-refresh), font_size_adjustments (if any text sizes change), image_handling (responsive images, aspect ratio changes). Create a responsive decision matrix: for each reusable pattern (data table, form, card grid, navigation), define the breakpoint behavior once and reference it from screens.
steps.done_when: responsive_spec exists with per-screen responsive rules and reusable responsive patterns.

steps.name: accessibility_specification
steps.instruction: Define accessibility implementation details per screen. For each screen: focus_order (tab sequence through interactive elements, ordered list), aria_labels (for every interactive element that lacks visible text: icon buttons, image links, decorative vs informative images), aria_live_regions (dynamic content areas that should announce changes: notification count, form validation, search results count), keyboard_shortcuts (per-screen keyboard interactions beyond tab/enter), skip_links (skip to main content, skip to navigation), landmark_roles (header, nav, main, complementary, footer regions), heading_hierarchy (h1 through h6 must be sequential, one h1 per page), form_accessibility (labels linked to inputs, fieldset/legend for groups, error announcements, required field indication), color_independence (information not conveyed by color alone: icons, text, patterns as supplements).
steps.done_when: accessibility_spec exists with per-screen focus order, ARIA requirements, and keyboard interaction details.

steps.name: design_spec_review
steps.instruction: Review all design specifications for: 1) Coverage (every user story has corresponding screens, every screen has responsive and accessibility specs), 2) Consistency (same patterns used for same purposes across screens, token usage is consistent), 3) Component reuse (are custom one-off elements used where existing components would work?), 4) Accessibility completeness (every screen has focus order, every interactive element has accessible name), 5) Responsive completeness (every screen has mobile, tablet, desktop behavior defined), 6) Implementability (is every spec detailed enough for an implementer agent to code without guessing?), 7) Token compliance (all colors, sizes, spacing reference design tokens, no magic numbers). Output: status, issues with spec references.
steps.done_when: design_spec_review_result exists with comprehensive design quality assessment.

steps.name: design_spec_visual_review
steps.instruction: If any visual mockups, screenshots, or reference images exist at this point, review the design specifications against them for visual fidelity. If no visual references exist yet, evaluate the design token choices and screen specifications for: color_harmony (do the chosen colors work well together?), typography_hierarchy (is the type scale clear and readable?), spacing_consistency (does the spacing feel balanced?), competitive_positioning (based on competitor_ux_evaluation, will our design feel modern and competitive?), visual_coherence (do all the specs together feel like one product?). Output findings and recommendations.
steps.done_when: visual_review_result exists with visual quality assessment and improvement recommendations.

steps.name: design_handoff_document
steps.instruction: Compile all design artifacts into a single Design Handoff document structured for implementer consumption. Structure: 1) Design Tokens (complete token reference), 2) Component Library (every component spec, alphabetically), 3) Screen Specifications (organized by flow: auth screens, onboarding screens, core feature screens, settings screens, error screens), 4) Interaction Guide (all animations and transitions), 5) Responsive Guide (breakpoint rules and patterns), 6) Accessibility Guide (ARIA patterns, focus management rules, keyboard shortcuts). Include a quick-reference cheat sheet at the top: most-used tokens, component import names, common patterns. Address any issues from design_spec_review_result. Version the document.
steps.done_when: design_handoff exists as the single source of truth for all UI implementation.

steps.name: epic_definition
steps.instruction: Group user stories into epics. Each epic: epic_id (EPIC-001...), name, description, user_stories (list of story IDs), dependencies (which other epics must be completed first), estimated_total_effort (sum of story estimates), priority_order (which epic to build first). Standard epics to consider: project_setup (Sprint 0), authentication, onboarding, core_feature_1, core_feature_2, ..., notifications, search, settings, billing, admin. Order epics by: dependency (foundation first), value (highest value features early), risk (technically risky features early to de-risk). The epic ordering defines the overall build sequence.
steps.done_when: epics exists with all stories grouped, dependencies mapped, and build order established.

steps.name: work_breakdown
steps.instruction: Break every user story into implementation tasks. For each story, generate tasks following this pattern: task_type → agent mapping: db_migration (if schema change needed) → implementer, backend_model (data model/entity) → implementer, backend_service (business logic) → implementer, backend_endpoint (API route) → implementer, backend_middleware (if needed) → implementer, backend_unit_tests → test_generator, backend_integration_tests → test_generator, frontend_types (TypeScript types) → implementer, frontend_state (store/slice) → implementer, frontend_api_client (API integration) → implementer, frontend_components (UI) → implementer, frontend_page (page assembly) → implementer, frontend_form_handling (if forms) → implementer, frontend_states (loading/error/empty) → implementer, frontend_responsive → implementer, frontend_accessibility → implementer, frontend_unit_tests → test_generator, frontend_component_tests → test_generator, e2e_test → test_generator, code_review → reviewer, visual_review → visual_reviewer. Each task: task_id, story_id, epic_id, title, agent, description, depends_on_tasks, estimated_effort (XS/S/M/L).
steps.done_when: work_breakdown exists with every story decomposed into agent-assignable tasks with dependencies and estimates.

steps.name: dependency_graph
steps.instruction: Build a complete task dependency graph. For every task, verify and finalize: depends_on (list of task_ids that must complete before this task can start), blocks (list of task_ids that cannot start until this task completes). Identify: critical_path (the longest chain of dependent tasks — this determines minimum project duration), parallelizable_groups (sets of tasks with no mutual dependencies that can run simultaneously), bottleneck_tasks (tasks that block many other tasks — these should be prioritized). Output the graph as an adjacency list and identify the critical path with total estimated duration.
steps.done_when: dependency_graph exists with verified dependencies, critical path identified, and parallel groups marked.

steps.name: effort_estimation
steps.instruction: Refine effort estimates for every task. For each task: task_id, estimated_effort (XS: <30min, S: 30min-2hr, M: 2-6hr, L: 6-16hr, XL: 16-40hr), confidence (high/medium/low), complexity_factors (what makes this easy or hard: new technology, complex logic, many edge cases, integration dependency). Flag any XL tasks for further decomposition. Calculate: total_estimated_hours (using midpoints: XS=0.25, S=1, M=4, L=11, XL=28), per_epic_total, per_sprint_capacity_estimate (assume 40 productive hours per sprint if serial, more if parallelizable). Identify: riskiest_estimates (low confidence tasks — these may blow up).
steps.done_when: effort_estimates exists with per-task estimates, totals, and risky estimates flagged.

steps.name: risk_assessment
steps.instruction: Identify and assess project risks. Risk categories: technical (new technology, complex algorithms, performance uncertainty), integration (third-party API changes, rate limits, downtime), scope (feature creep, underestimated complexity, unclear requirements), dependency (tasks blocking many others, single points of failure in the plan), security (data breach risk, auth implementation correctness), operational (deployment failure, data migration risk). For each risk: risk_id, description, category, likelihood (1-5), impact (1-5), risk_score (likelihood × impact), early_warning_signs (how we'll know this risk is materializing), mitigation_strategy (what we do to reduce likelihood), contingency_plan (what we do if it happens), owner_agent (which agent type handles this risk).
steps.done_when: risk_assessment exists with all risks scored, with mitigation and contingency plans.

steps.name: sprint_0_planning
steps.instruction: Plan Sprint 0 (setup sprint). Include all tasks that must complete before feature development begins: repository setup, CI/CD pipeline, Docker configuration, backend scaffolding, frontend scaffolding, design system implementation (tokens + primitive components + composite components), database setup, testing framework setup, staging environment provisioning, developer documentation. Order by dependencies. Verify total effort fits within one sprint (if not, split into Sprint 0a and 0b). Output: sprint_id, sprint_name, task_list (ordered), total_estimated_hours, sprint_goal ('All infrastructure and tooling ready for feature development. Design system components implemented and documented.').
steps.done_when: sprint_0_plan exists with ordered task list and verified fit within sprint capacity.

steps.name: feature_sprint_planning
steps.instruction: Plan all feature sprints after Sprint 0. For each sprint: sprint_id, sprint_name, sprint_goal (what user-facing capability is delivered), task_list (ordered by dependency), epic_coverage (which epics are progressed), total_estimated_hours, definition_of_done_for_sprint (what must be true for sprint to be complete). Constraints: respect dependency_graph (no task before its dependencies), each sprint should deliver a demonstrable increment (not just backend with no UI, or UI with no backend), balance frontend and backend work, keep sprint size consistent. Add buffer: plan to 80% capacity to absorb estimation errors. Include review and testing tasks within each sprint, not as separate sprints.
steps.done_when: sprint_plans exists with all sprints defined, each delivering a working increment.

steps.name: milestone_definition
steps.instruction: Define project milestones with clear criteria. Milestones: alpha (criteria: core feature works end-to-end for primary persona, auth complete, basic error handling, deployed to staging — sprints included: list), beta (criteria: all MVP features implemented, tested, design polished, performance acceptable — sprints included), release_candidate (criteria: all bugs fixed to accepted threshold, security audit passed, documentation complete, production infrastructure ready — sprints included), general_availability (criteria: launched to public, monitoring active, support processes in place, launch marketing executed). For each: milestone_name, target_sprint, entry_criteria (what must be true to declare this milestone reached), exit_criteria (what must be true to proceed to next milestone), risks_to_timeline.
steps.done_when: milestones exists with 4 milestones, each with clear entry/exit criteria tied to specific sprints.

steps.name: definition_of_done
steps.instruction: Define Definition of Done (DoD) at multiple levels. Task DoD: code written, code compiles/builds without errors, linter passes, type checker passes, relevant tests written and passing, self-reviewed. Story DoD: all tasks complete, all acceptance criteria verified by tests, code reviewed by reviewer agent, no critical/high bugs, documentation updated, deployed to staging, visual_reviewer has checked UI if applicable. Sprint DoD: all committed stories meet Story DoD, full regression suite passes, no critical bugs outstanding, staging environment is stable, sprint goal is demonstrably achieved. Release DoD: all sprint DoDs met, security audit passed, performance targets met, documentation complete, legal documents in place, production infrastructure ready.
steps.done_when: definition_of_done exists with DoD for task, story, sprint, and release levels.

steps.name: project_plan_review
steps.instruction: Review the complete project plan. Check: 1) All user stories are assigned to a sprint, 2) Dependencies are respected in sprint ordering, 3) Sprint loads are balanced (no sprint is 2x another), 4) Milestones are realistic given estimates, 5) Risk mitigations are actually planned as tasks (not just documented), 6) Critical path tasks are not in late sprints, 7) Testing and review tasks are included in every sprint (not deferred), 8) Buffer exists for estimation errors, 9) DoD is achievable and not aspirational. If total timeline seems unrealistic for MVP scope, trigger needs_clarification presenting: estimated timeline, scope options (cut features to ship faster), and ask human for direction.
steps.done_when: project_plan_review_result exists. Timeline is realistic or human has approved adjusted scope.

steps.name: repository_initialization
steps.instruction: Create the Git repository. Execute: initialize git repo, create main branch, create develop branch, set develop as default branch, configure branch protection on main (require PR, require CI pass, no direct push), configure branch protection on develop (require PR, require CI pass). Create initial commit with empty README.md. Output: repository_url, branch_configuration, protection_rules_applied.
steps.done_when: Git repository exists with main and develop branches, both protected, develop set as default.

steps.name: git_configuration
steps.instruction: Create Git configuration files. Files to create: .gitignore (comprehensive for the chosen tech stack: node_modules, .env, dist, coverage, .DS_Store, IDE files, OS files, build artifacts, cache directories), .gitattributes (LF line endings for code, binary handling for images/fonts), .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (sections: Description, Type of Change checkboxes, Testing Done, Screenshots if applicable, Checklist), .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md (steps to reproduce, expected, actual, environment), .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md (user story format), CODEOWNERS (map directories to responsible agent roles), .github/COMMIT_CONVENTION.md (conventional commits: feat, fix, chore, docs, style, refactor, test, ci).
steps.done_when: All git configuration files exist and are committed to the repository.

steps.name: code_formatter_setup
steps.instruction: Install and configure the code formatter for the chosen stack. For JavaScript/TypeScript projects: install prettier, create .prettierrc (printWidth: 100, tabWidth: 2, useTabs: false, semi: true, singleQuote: true, trailingComma: 'all', bracketSpacing: true, arrowParens: 'always'), create .prettierignore (dist, coverage, node_modules, *.min.js, package-lock.json). For Python: install black + isort, configure in pyproject.toml. For Go: gofmt is built-in. Add format check script to package.json or equivalent: 'format': runs formatter, 'format:check': checks without modifying (for CI).
steps.done_when: Formatter is installed, configured, and format/format:check scripts are runnable.

steps.name: linter_setup
steps.instruction: Install and configure the linter. For TypeScript projects: install eslint + typescript-eslint + eslint-config-prettier (to avoid formatter conflicts), create eslint.config.js with rules: no-unused-vars (error), no-explicit-any (warn), consistent-return, no-console (warn in production), prefer-const, no-var. Enable recommended rulesets for the framework (e.g., react/recommended, react-hooks/recommended). Create .eslintignore (dist, node_modules, coverage). Add lint scripts: 'lint': runs linter, 'lint:fix': auto-fixes. For Python: install ruff, configure in pyproject.toml. Ensure linter and formatter don't conflict.
steps.done_when: Linter is installed, configured, non-conflicting with formatter, and lint/lint:fix scripts are runnable.

steps.name: precommit_hooks
steps.instruction: Set up pre-commit hooks to enforce code quality on every commit. For Node.js: install husky + lint-staged. Configure lint-staged in package.json: '*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}': ['eslint --fix', 'prettier --write'], '*.{json,md,yml,yaml,css,scss}': ['prettier --write']. Set up husky: 'prepare' script to install hooks, pre-commit hook runs lint-staged, commit-msg hook validates conventional commit format (install commitlint + @commitlint/config-conventional). For Python: install pre-commit, create .pre-commit-config.yaml with ruff and black hooks. Verify: make a commit with a linting error — it should be auto-fixed or blocked.
steps.done_when: Pre-commit hooks are installed and verified to block bad code and auto-fix formatting.

steps.name: editor_configuration
steps.instruction: Create editor configuration files. Files: .editorconfig (root=true, indent_style=space, indent_size=2 for js/ts/json/yaml, indent_size=4 for python, end_of_line=lf, charset=utf-8, trim_trailing_whitespace=true, insert_final_newline=true), .vscode/settings.json (editor.formatOnSave: true, editor.defaultFormatter for file types, editor.codeActionsOnSave: source.fixAll.eslint, typescript.preferences.importModuleSpecifier: 'non-relative'), .vscode/extensions.json (recommended: eslint, prettier, tailwindcss if using, editorconfig, gitlens, error-lens).
steps.done_when: Editor configuration files exist for consistent development environment.

steps.name: backend_project_init
steps.instruction: Initialize the backend project. Create the directory structure based on the architecture (if monorepo: /apps/api or /packages/api, if separate repo: root). Create folder structure: src/ (main source), src/config/ (configuration loading, env validation), src/routes/ or src/controllers/ (API route handlers), src/services/ (business logic), src/models/ or src/entities/ (data models), src/middleware/ (express/fastify middleware), src/utils/ (shared utilities), src/types/ (TypeScript type definitions), src/jobs/ (background job processors), src/emails/ (email templates). Create: src/index.ts or src/main.ts (application entry point with graceful shutdown handling), src/app.ts (application factory: creates and configures the server instance), src/config/env.ts (loads and validates all environment variables with clear error messages for missing required vars). Install core dependencies for the chosen framework.
steps.done_when: Backend project compiles, the server starts and listens on a port, graceful shutdown works.

steps.name: backend_typescript_config
steps.instruction: Configure TypeScript (or equivalent type system) for the backend. Create tsconfig.json with: strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, noImplicitReturns: true, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true, esModuleInterop: true, resolveJsonModule: true, moduleResolution appropriate for the runtime, target appropriate for Node version, path aliases (@/ → src/, @config/ → src/config/, @routes/ → src/routes/, etc.), outDir: dist, rootDir: src, include: ['src/**/*'], exclude: ['node_modules', 'dist', '**/*.test.ts']. Also create tsconfig.build.json (extends base, excludes test files) for production builds. Add build script and verify: 'tsc --noEmit' succeeds.
steps.done_when: TypeScript compiles with strict mode, path aliases resolve, build produces output in dist/.

steps.name: env_management
steps.instruction: Set up environment variable management. Create .env.example with every variable the application needs (NO actual secrets, only structure): DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/dbname, REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379, JWT_SECRET=your-secret-here, JWT_EXPIRY=15m, REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRY=7d, SMTP_HOST=, SMTP_PORT=, SMTP_USER=, SMTP_PASS=, STORAGE_BUCKET=, STORAGE_REGION=, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=, APP_URL=http://localhost:3000, API_URL=http://localhost:4000, NODE_ENV=development, LOG_LEVEL=debug, PORT=4000. Create src/config/env.ts that: loads dotenv, validates every required variable exists at startup (crash immediately with clear message if missing), exports typed config object, separates required vs optional variables.
steps.done_when: .env.example documents all variables, env.ts validates and exports typed config, missing vars crash with clear message.

steps.name: base_middleware_setup
steps.instruction: Implement the base middleware stack per the architecture design. Create and wire up in order: request_id_middleware (generate UUID for every request, attach to request context, include in all log entries), cors_middleware (configure origins from env, allowed methods, allowed headers, credentials), body_parser_middleware (JSON body with size limit from requirements), compression_middleware (gzip responses above threshold), request_logger_middleware (log: method, path, status_code, duration_ms, request_id, user_id if authenticated — using structured JSON format from logging_strategy), security_headers_middleware (X-Request-Id in response, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY). Wire all middleware in app.ts in the correct order.
steps.done_when: All base middleware is implemented and wired. A request logs correctly with request_id and duration.

steps.name: error_handling_middleware
steps.instruction: Implement the global error handling system per error_handling_strategy. Create: AppError class (extends Error, has: statusCode, errorCode from taxonomy, isOperational flag, details array), error_handler_middleware (catches all errors: if AppError → format per error_response_format with appropriate status code; if unknown error → log full stack trace, return generic 500 with request_id for debugging, never expose stack traces to client), not_found_handler (catches requests to undefined routes, returns 404 with helpful message), validation_error_handler (formats validation library errors into field-level error format), async_handler (utility wrapper that catches async errors and passes to error middleware). Create specific error factory functions: notFound(resource), unauthorized(message), forbidden(message), badRequest(details), conflict(message), rateLimited(), internalError().
steps.done_when: Error handling middleware catches all error types, formats them consistently, and never leaks internal details.

steps.name: logger_setup
steps.instruction: Implement the structured logger per logging_strategy. Create src/utils/logger.ts: configure logging library (pino for Node.js, structlog for Python), structured JSON output with fields: timestamp (ISO 8601), level, message, request_id (from context), user_id (from context), service (app name), action (what operation), duration_ms (for timed operations), metadata (additional context). Create log levels: debug, info, warn, error, fatal. Create PII scrubber: before logging any object, redact fields matching: password, token, secret, authorization, credit_card, ssn, api_key. Create child logger factory: logger.child({request_id, user_id}) for per-request logging. In development: pretty-print logs for readability. In production: JSON only.
steps.done_when: Logger produces structured JSON logs, scrubs PII, supports child loggers, and pretty-prints in development.

steps.name: database_client_setup
steps.instruction: Set up the database client/ORM. Install the chosen ORM (Prisma, Drizzle, TypeORM, SQLAlchemy, etc.). Configure: connection from DATABASE_URL environment variable, connection pool settings (min: 2, max: 10 for development, configurable for production), query logging in development mode (log SQL queries and execution time), connection timeout (5 seconds), idle timeout (10 seconds). Create src/config/database.ts: exports configured client instance, exports a health check function that runs a simple query (SELECT 1) and returns true/false. Verify: client connects to a local database (Docker or local install), health check passes.
steps.done_when: Database client connects, queries work, health check passes, connection pooling is configured.

steps.name: migration_setup
steps.instruction: Set up the database migration system and create the initial migration. Configure the migration tool from the chosen ORM. Create the initial migration that implements the complete database_schema: all tables, columns, types, constraints, indexes, enums, foreign keys. Add migration scripts to package.json: 'db:migrate' (run pending migrations), 'db:migrate:rollback' (rollback last migration), 'db:migrate:status' (show migration status), 'db:migrate:create' (create new empty migration), 'db:reset' (drop all tables, re-run all migrations, re-seed). Run the initial migration. Verify: all tables exist with correct schema, all indexes exist, all constraints work (test a foreign key violation).
steps.done_when: Migration system is configured, initial migration creates complete schema, migration scripts work.

steps.name: seed_data
steps.instruction: Create a database seed script with realistic sample data. Create src/database/seed.ts: populate all tables with representative data. Include: at least 3 user accounts (one per persona, with different roles: admin, regular user, free tier user), realistic data for all core entities (not 'test123' but plausible content), enough data to demonstrate list views with pagination (at least 25 items for main entities), edge cases (user with no data, user at plan limits, entity with maximum fields filled). Use consistent references between related data. Hash passwords with the production hashing algorithm. Add seed script to package.json: 'db:seed'. Make seed idempotent (can be run multiple times without duplicating data — use upsert or clear-then-insert).
steps.done_when: Seed script creates realistic data for all entities, is idempotent, and runs without errors.

steps.name: health_check_endpoint
steps.instruction: Implement the /health endpoint per monitoring_strategy. The endpoint should: require no authentication, return JSON with: status ('ok' or 'degraded' or 'unhealthy'), version (from package.json), uptime_seconds, timestamp, checks (object with each dependency: database: {status, latency_ms}, cache: {status, latency_ms}, storage: {status} — only check dependencies that exist). Health logic: if all checks pass → 200 with status 'ok', if non-critical check fails → 200 with status 'degraded', if critical check (database) fails → 503 with status 'unhealthy'. Also create /ready endpoint (returns 200 only when fully ready to serve traffic — for Kubernetes readiness probes). Set appropriate Cache-Control: no-cache headers.
steps.done_when: Health endpoint returns correct status based on dependency health. Ready endpoint works.

steps.name: api_validation_setup
steps.instruction: Set up request validation. Install validation library (zod for TypeScript, pydantic for Python). Create shared validation schemas for common patterns: pagination_schema (page: integer min 1, limit: integer min 1 max 100, default 20), id_param_schema (validates UUID or expected ID format), sort_schema (field: enum of allowed fields, order: 'asc' | 'desc'), date_range_schema (start: ISO date, end: ISO date, end > start). Create validation middleware that: parses request against schema (body, query, params separately), on validation failure: returns 422 with field-level errors in the standard format from error_handling_strategy, on success: attaches typed, validated data to request. Create example usage in a test route.
steps.done_when: Validation library configured, common schemas created, validation middleware returns typed data on success and formatted errors on failure.

steps.name: backend_test_setup
steps.instruction: Set up the backend testing framework. Install: test runner (vitest or jest), assertion library (built-in or chai), mocking library (built-in or sinon), supertest (for HTTP testing). Create config: test environment variables (.env.test with test database URL), setup file (connects to test database, runs migrations, global setup/teardown), test utilities (createTestUser helper, authenticatedRequest helper that includes auth headers, resetDatabase helper that truncates tables between tests). Create test directory structure: tests/unit/ (service layer tests), tests/integration/ (API endpoint tests), tests/e2e/ (full flow tests). Write one smoke test: 'GET /health returns 200 with status ok'. Add scripts: 'test' (run all), 'test:unit', 'test:integration', 'test:watch', 'test:coverage'.
steps.done_when: Test framework is configured, test database works, helper utilities exist, smoke test passes.

steps.name: frontend_project_init
steps.instruction: Initialize the frontend project using the chosen framework's CLI (create-next-app, create-vite, etc.). Create folder structure per frontend_architecture: src/app/ or src/pages/ (route pages), src/components/ (reusable components, subdivided: src/components/primitives/, src/components/composite/, src/components/layout/, src/components/features/), src/hooks/ (custom hooks), src/stores/ or src/state/ (state management), src/lib/ or src/utils/ (utilities, formatters, constants), src/types/ (TypeScript type definitions), src/styles/ (global styles, CSS variables), src/assets/ (static assets: icons, images, fonts), src/api/ (API client functions). Create placeholder index files in each directory. Remove framework boilerplate/demo content.
steps.done_when: Frontend project runs in development mode, shows a blank page, has the complete folder structure.

steps.name: frontend_typescript_config
steps.instruction: Configure TypeScript for the frontend. Update tsconfig.json: strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, path aliases (@/ → src/, @components/ → src/components/, @hooks/ → src/hooks/, @lib/ → src/lib/, @api/ → src/api/, @stores/ → src/stores/, @types/ → src/types/, @assets/ → src/assets/), jsx: appropriate setting for framework. Ensure path aliases also work with the bundler (Vite: update vite.config.ts resolve.alias, Next.js: works automatically with tsconfig paths). Verify: import a module using a path alias, confirm it resolves in the editor and at build time.
steps.done_when: TypeScript strict mode compiles, path aliases resolve in editor and build.

steps.name: routing_setup
steps.instruction: Configure the frontend router. Create src/routes/index.ts (or equivalent for the framework): define every route from information_architecture sitemap as a constant (export const ROUTES = { HOME: '/', LOGIN: '/login', DASHBOARD: '/dashboard', ... }). Set up route definitions with: path, component (lazy loaded with React.lazy or framework equivalent), layout (which layout component wraps this page), auth_required (boolean), allowed_roles (if role-restricted). Create route guard component: checks if user is authenticated, if not redirect to login with return URL, checks role if role-restricted, redirects to 403 if unauthorized. Create placeholder page components for every route (just the page name as heading). Verify: all routes resolve, unauthenticated routes show content, authenticated routes redirect to login placeholder.
steps.done_when: All routes from sitemap are defined as constants and route definitions, lazy loading works, auth guard redirects correctly.

steps.name: state_management_setup
steps.instruction: Set up the state management solution per frontend_architecture. Install chosen library (Zustand, Redux Toolkit, Pinia, Jotai, etc.). Create: src/stores/auth.store.ts (skeleton: user state, isAuthenticated computed, login/logout actions — implementation later), src/stores/ui.store.ts (sidebar open/closed, theme preference, notification toast queue). If using server state library (React Query, SWR, Apollo): install and configure query client with defaults: staleTime (5 minutes for most queries), retry (3 times with exponential backoff), refetchOnWindowFocus (true in production), error handling (global error handler that shows toast). Create devtools integration for state debugging in development.
steps.done_when: State management is configured with auth and UI stores, server state library configured with defaults and devtools.

steps.name: api_client_setup
steps.instruction: Create the base API client. Create src/api/client.ts: configure HTTP client (axios or fetch wrapper) with: baseURL from environment variable, default headers (Content-Type: application/json, Accept: application/json), request interceptor (attach JWT access token from auth store to Authorization header), response interceptor (on 401: attempt silent token refresh using refresh token, if refresh succeeds retry original request, if refresh fails redirect to login and clear auth state), error interceptor (transform API errors into typed error objects matching error_handling_strategy format), timeout (30 seconds default). Create src/api/types.ts: ApiResponse<T> generic type, ApiError type, PaginatedResponse<T> type. Create src/api/helpers.ts: buildQueryString utility for filters/pagination/sorting.
steps.done_when: API client handles auth headers, token refresh, error transformation, and request helpers are available.

steps.name: design_token_implementation
steps.instruction: Implement all design tokens from design_tokens specification as code. Create src/styles/tokens.css (CSS custom properties): every color token as --color-primary-50 through --color-primary-950, etc., every spacing token as --spacing-1 through --spacing-24, every font size, weight, line height, every shadow, border radius, z-index, transition duration and easing. If using Tailwind CSS: configure tailwind.config.ts to use these exact token values (extend theme with custom colors, spacing, etc.). If using CSS-in-JS: create a theme object that exports all tokens as a typed JavaScript object. Create src/styles/globals.css: CSS reset (or normalize), base element styles (body font, background, text color using tokens), utility classes for common patterns. Verify: tokens are accessible from any component, changing a token value propagates everywhere.
steps.done_when: All design tokens are implemented as CSS variables or theme config, globally accessible from any component.

steps.name: base_layout_implementation
steps.instruction: Implement the base layout components per layout_system_spec. Create: AppShell component (the outermost layout: renders header, optional sidebar, main content area, applies min-height: 100vh), AuthLayout component (centered card layout for login/signup pages, no sidebar/header), DashboardLayout component (with header + sidebar + content area, sidebar collapsible), ContentContainer component (max-width wrapper with horizontal padding, centered), PageHeader component (page title, optional subtitle, optional action buttons area). Each layout must: use design tokens for all spacing and colors, be responsive per layout_system_spec breakpoints, accept children for content areas. Wire layouts to routes: auth routes use AuthLayout, dashboard routes use DashboardLayout.
steps.done_when: Layout components render correctly at all breakpoints, routes use correct layouts, sidebar collapses on mobile.

steps.name: primitive_button
steps.instruction: Implement the Button component exactly per component_spec_primitives specification. Implement every variant (primary, secondary, ghost, danger, link), every size (sm, md, lg), every state (default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading). Props: variant, size, disabled, loading (shows spinner and disables), leftIcon, rightIcon, fullWidth, type (button/submit/reset), onClick, children. Accessibility: render as <button> element, disabled attribute when disabled or loading, aria-busy when loading, focus-visible ring using design tokens. Loading state: show spinner, keep button width stable (don't shrink when text is replaced). Use design tokens for all colors, spacing, border-radius, font-size, transition.
steps.done_when: Button renders all variants × sizes × states correctly, is keyboard accessible, loading state is stable.

steps.name: primitive_input
steps.instruction: Implement the Input component per component_spec_primitives. Props: type (text, email, password, number, search, url, tel), size (sm, md, lg), label, placeholder, helperText, errorMessage, required, disabled, leftIcon, rightIcon, clearable (show X button when has value), onClear. States: default, hover (border color change), focus (ring using design tokens), error (red border + error message below), disabled (reduced opacity, no interaction). For password type: toggle show/hide button as rightIcon. Accessibility: label element linked via htmlFor/id, aria-describedby pointing to helper or error text, aria-invalid when error, aria-required when required. Render: label above, input with optional icons, helper/error text below.
steps.done_when: Input renders all types and states, label-input linkage works for screen readers, error state displays correctly.

steps.name: primitive_typography
steps.instruction: Implement typography components per design spec. Create: Heading component (props: level 1-6, renders correct h1-h6 tag, applies correct font-size/weight/line-height from design tokens), Text component (props: size xs-xl, weight regular/medium/semibold/bold, color primary/secondary/tertiary/disabled, as: element to render as — default p, supports span/div/label), Code component (inline code styling with mono font), Caption component (small helper text). All components accept className for extension. All use design tokens. Verify: heading hierarchy is visually distinct, text sizes form a clear scale.
steps.done_when: Typography components render correct semantic HTML with design token styles.

steps.name: primitive_remaining
steps.instruction: Implement remaining primitive components per component_spec_primitives: Checkbox (props: checked, indeterminate, label, disabled, onChange — renders hidden native checkbox + custom visual, keyboard: space to toggle), Radio (props: value, checked, name, label, disabled — works in RadioGroup), Toggle/Switch (props: checked, label, disabled — animated slide), Select (props: options[], value, placeholder, label, error, disabled — native select or custom dropdown per spec), Textarea (props: rows, maxLength, label, error, resize — with character counter if maxLength), Link (props: href, external: opens new tab with rel=noopener, active state for nav links), Badge (props: variant info/success/warning/error, size sm/md), Avatar (props: src, name, size — image with fallback to initials with color derived from name), Divider, Spinner (props: size sm/md/lg), Skeleton (props: width, height, variant text/circle/rect — with shimmer animation).
steps.done_when: All primitive components from spec are implemented with correct props, states, and accessibility.

steps.name: composite_card
steps.instruction: Implement the Card component per component_spec_composite. Composed of: Card (container with shadow, border, border-radius from tokens), Card.Header (optional: title, subtitle, action area for buttons/dropdown), Card.Body (content area with padding), Card.Footer (optional: action buttons, aligned right). Props: variant (default with border, elevated with shadow), clickable (adds hover effect and cursor pointer, renders as button or link), padding (none/sm/md/lg). Hover state for clickable: slight shadow increase + border color change. Accessibility: if clickable, ensure it has a role and keyboard interaction.
steps.done_when: Card renders with header/body/footer composition, clickable variant has hover and keyboard support.

steps.name: composite_modal
steps.instruction: Implement the Modal/Dialog component per component_spec_composite. Structure: overlay (semi-transparent backdrop, click to close if closeable), container (centered, width sizes: sm/md/lg/xl/full), Modal.Header (title, optional close X button), Modal.Body (scrollable content area if content overflows), Modal.Footer (action buttons, typically cancel + confirm). Behavior: focus trap (tab cycles within modal only), initial focus on first focusable element or close button, escape key closes, scroll lock on body while open, animation: fade in overlay + scale up container (per interaction_spec timing). Props: isOpen, onClose, size, closeOnOverlayClick, closeOnEscape, title. Accessibility: role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby pointing to title.
steps.done_when: Modal renders with overlay, traps focus, handles escape/overlay click, animates open/close, locks body scroll.

steps.name: composite_toast
steps.instruction: Implement Toast notification system per component_spec_composite. Create: ToastProvider (wraps app, manages toast queue in state), useToast hook (returns: toast.success(message), toast.error(message), toast.warning(message), toast.info(message) — each optionally takes: duration, action button), ToastContainer (renders toast stack, positioned top-right per spec), Toast (individual: icon per variant, message, optional action button, close X, auto-dismiss progress bar). Behavior: new toasts stack below existing, max 5 visible (oldest auto-dismissed), slide-in animation from right (per interaction_spec), auto-dismiss after duration (default: success 3s, error 5s, info 3s, warning 4s), hover pauses auto-dismiss timer. Alert/Banner: separate component for inline persistent alerts with dismiss option.
steps.done_when: Toast system works via hook, stacks correctly, auto-dismisses, hover pauses timer, animations work.

steps.name: composite_remaining
steps.instruction: Implement remaining composite components per component_spec_composite: Dropdown/Menu (trigger button, menu items with icon/label/shortcut, dividers, keyboard: arrow keys navigate, enter selects, escape closes), Tooltip (trigger: hover after 300ms delay + focus, positioned with auto-flip, arrow pointing to trigger), Popover (trigger: click, positioned with auto-flip, close on outside click/escape), Tabs (horizontal variant, controlled: activeTab prop + onChange, lazy load tab content, sync with URL hash if specified), Accordion (single/multi expand mode, animated height transition, chevron rotation indicator), Table (sortable column headers with sort indicator, responsive: horizontal scroll on mobile per responsive_spec, row hover highlight), Pagination (page numbers with ellipsis, prev/next buttons, items per page selector, total count display), Breadcrumb (separator, last item non-clickable), EmptyState (icon/illustration area, heading, description, CTA button), Navbar, Sidebar (collapsible, active item highlight, nested groups).
steps.done_when: All composite components from spec are implemented with keyboard navigation and accessibility.

steps.name: component_playground
steps.instruction: Set up Storybook (or equivalent component playground) and create stories for every implemented component. Install Storybook for the chosen framework. For each component, create a story file that demonstrates: all variants (one story per variant), all sizes, interactive controls (Storybook args for all props), composition examples (e.g., Card with Button in footer, Modal with form inside). Add a 'Design Tokens' documentation page showing all colors, typography, spacing as visual swatches. Add an 'Icons' page if using an icon set. Configure Storybook addons: a11y (accessibility checker), viewport (responsive testing). Verify: Storybook builds and runs, every component has at least one story.
steps.done_when: Storybook runs, every component has stories showing all variants, a11y addon is active.

steps.name: frontend_test_setup
steps.instruction: Set up frontend testing framework. Install: test runner (vitest or jest), component testing library (@testing-library/react or framework equivalent), user-event library (@testing-library/user-event for realistic interaction simulation), jsdom environment. Configure: test environment (jsdom), setup file (configure testing library, add custom matchers like toBeInTheDocument, mock next/router or equivalent), path alias resolution in tests (same aliases as main code), CSS module/style mocking. Create test utilities: src/test/utils.tsx (custom render function that wraps components with providers: theme, router, state store, toast provider), src/test/mocks/ (mock API responses, mock router). Write one smoke test: render the Button component, verify it's in the document. Add scripts: 'test:ui' (unit + component tests), 'test:ui:watch', 'test:ui:coverage'.
steps.done_when: Frontend test framework configured, custom render with providers works, smoke test passes.

steps.name: docker_backend
steps.instruction: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile for the backend. Stage 1 (deps): FROM node:lts-alpine, WORKDIR, copy package.json + lockfile, run npm ci. Stage 2 (build): copy source, run build (tsc). Stage 3 (production): FROM node:lts-alpine, WORKDIR, copy package.json + lockfile, run npm ci --production, copy built output from build stage, create non-root user, switch to non-root user, EXPOSE port, HEALTHCHECK (curl health endpoint), CMD to start the server. Optimize: use .dockerignore (node_modules, .git, tests, docs, .env), layer caching (deps before source), minimal final image (only production deps + built code). Verify: docker build succeeds, docker run starts the server, health check passes inside container.
steps.done_when: Backend Dockerfile builds, runs, and passes health check in container.

steps.name: docker_frontend
steps.instruction: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile for the frontend. Stage 1 (deps): FROM node:lts-alpine, install deps. Stage 2 (build): copy source, run build (produces static files). Stage 3 (serve): FROM nginx:alpine, copy built static files to nginx html directory, copy custom nginx.conf (gzip on, SPA routing: try_files $uri /index.html for client-side routing, cache headers: long cache for hashed assets, no-cache for index.html, security headers). Use .dockerignore. For Next.js/SSR apps: adjust stage 3 to run Node.js instead of nginx. Verify: docker build succeeds, docker run serves the app, client-side routing works (direct URL access to /dashboard returns the app, not 404).
steps.done_when: Frontend Dockerfile builds, serves static files with correct routing and caching headers.

steps.name: docker_compose
steps.instruction: Create docker-compose.yml for local development. Services: api (build from backend Dockerfile, ports: map host port to container port, volumes: mount src/ for hot reload, environment: from .env, depends_on: db and redis), web (build from frontend Dockerfile or use dev command, ports: map host port, volumes: mount src/ for hot reload, environment: API URL), db (PostgreSQL or chosen DB image, ports: map to non-standard host port to avoid conflicts, volumes: named volume for data persistence, environment: user/password/database from .env), redis (Redis image, ports: map to host). Optionally add: mailhog or mailpit (catches all outgoing email in development, web UI for viewing), minio (S3-compatible local storage). Add docker-compose.override.yml for development-specific settings. Add scripts: 'docker:up', 'docker:down', 'docker:logs'.
steps.done_when: docker-compose up starts all services, API connects to DB and Redis, frontend connects to API.

steps.name: ci_pipeline
steps.instruction: Create CI pipeline configuration. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml (or equivalent): trigger on push to any branch and on pull_request to develop and main. Jobs: lint (install deps, run eslint, run prettier check), typecheck (run tsc --noEmit for both backend and frontend), test_backend (start test database via service container, run migrations, run backend tests with coverage), test_frontend (run frontend unit and component tests with coverage), build (build backend, build frontend — verify both compile), security_audit (run npm audit or equivalent). Each job: uses caching for node_modules (cache key: hash of lockfile), runs on ubuntu-latest, fails fast on first error. Add status badges to README. Configure branch protection to require CI pass before merge.
steps.done_when: CI pipeline runs on PR, all stages pass, branch protection requires CI green.

steps.name: cd_pipeline_staging
steps.instruction: Create CD pipeline for staging deployment. Create .github/workflows/deploy-staging.yml: trigger on push to develop branch (after PR merge). Steps: run CI checks (or require CI workflow to pass first), build Docker images (tag with git SHA), push images to container registry (GitHub Container Registry, ECR, or equivalent), deploy to staging environment (method depends on infrastructure: update ECS task definition, kubectl apply, railway deploy, fly deploy, etc.), run database migrations on staging, run smoke test against staging URL (curl health endpoint, verify 200), notify on success/failure (post to Telegram or configured channel). Include: rollback step if smoke test fails (redeploy previous image). Store deployment metadata: git SHA, timestamp, deployer.
steps.done_when: Merge to develop automatically deploys to staging, runs smoke test, and notifies on result.

steps.name: cd_pipeline_production
steps.instruction: Create CD pipeline for production deployment. Create .github/workflows/deploy-production.yml: trigger on push of version tag (v*.*.*) or manual workflow dispatch with version input. Steps: verify tag exists on main branch only, run full CI suite, build Docker images (tag with version number + git SHA), push to container registry, deploy to production (same method as staging but targeting production environment), run database migrations, run comprehensive smoke test (health check + critical endpoints), if smoke test fails: automatic rollback to previous version and alert. Require manual approval step before deployment starts (GitHub environment protection rules). Store release metadata: version, git SHA, timestamp, changelog link.
steps.done_when: Production deployment triggered by version tag, requires approval, auto-rolls-back on failure.

steps.name: staging_provisioning
steps.instruction: Provision the staging environment on the chosen infrastructure platform. Create: compute resources (appropriately sized — smallest viable instance), database instance (smallest tier with automated backups), cache instance (smallest Redis tier), object storage bucket (for file uploads), environment variables (all from .env.example configured with staging values, real third-party API keys in test/sandbox mode). Configure: networking (HTTPS, domain: staging.yourdomain.com or equivalent), database connection from compute to database (private networking if available), container registry access. Output: staging_url, database_connection_verified, cache_connection_verified, deployment_method_documented.
steps.done_when: Staging environment is provisioned, all services are accessible, connections verified.

steps.name: staging_deployment_test
steps.instruction: Test the complete CI/CD pipeline end-to-end. Execute: push a commit to develop branch, verify CI pipeline triggers and passes, verify CD pipeline triggers and deploys to staging, verify application is accessible at staging URL, verify health endpoint returns 200 with all checks passing, verify database migrations ran (check schema matches expectations), verify frontend loads and renders correctly, verify API returns expected response for a test endpoint. If any step fails, diagnose and fix the pipeline configuration. Output: pipeline_execution_log, staging_url_accessible (true/false), health_check_result, issues_found_and_fixed.
steps.done_when: Full CI/CD pipeline works end-to-end: commit → build → deploy → health check passes.

steps.name: sprint_0_smoke_test
steps.instruction: Full validation of the complete Sprint 0 deliverables. Execute this checklist: 1) Clone repository from scratch, 2) Run npm install (or equivalent), verify no errors, 3) Run docker-compose up, verify all services start, 4) Run database migrations, verify schema created, 5) Run seed script, verify data populated, 6) Run linter, verify no errors, 7) Run type checker, verify no errors, 8) Run backend tests, verify all pass, 9) Run frontend tests, verify all pass, 10) Access frontend in browser, verify it loads, 11) Access API health endpoint, verify 200, 12) Build production Docker images, verify they build, 13) Access Storybook, verify it loads with all components, 14) Push a test commit, verify CI passes, 15) Merge to develop, verify staging deployment succeeds. Output: checklist with pass/fail per item, blocking_issues.
steps.done_when: All 15 checklist items pass, or blocking issues are documented for fixing.

steps.name: developer_workflow_docs
steps.instruction: Write comprehensive developer workflow documentation. Sections: Prerequisites (required software: Node.js version, Docker, Git), Quick Start (step-by-step from clone to running app: 5-10 steps maximum), Environment Setup (copy .env.example, explain each variable, how to get API keys for development), Running Locally (docker-compose up for services, npm run dev for app, accessing frontend and API URLs), Running Tests (how to run unit, integration, E2E tests, how to run a single test file, how to get coverage), Git Workflow (branch naming: feature/XXX, fix/XXX, from develop, PR to develop, squash merge), Commit Convention (conventional commits with examples), Code Style (auto-enforced by linter/formatter, just save and it formats), Creating a New Feature (checklist: create branch, implement backend, implement frontend, write tests, create PR), Deployment (staging: auto on merge to develop, production: tag a version), Troubleshooting (common issues: port in use, database connection failed, migration errors).
steps.done_when: developer_workflow_doc exists and a new developer agent could follow it to start contributing.

steps.name: sprint_0_review
steps.instruction: Review all Sprint 0 deliverables. Check: 1) Code quality of scaffolding (clean, well-organized, follows conventions), 2) All design system components match the spec (spot-check 5 components against component_spec), 3) CI/CD pipeline is reliable (not flaky), 4) Docker setup works on clean machine, 5) Test infrastructure catches real bugs (intentionally break something, verify tests catch it), 6) Documentation is accurate and followable, 7) No security issues in initial setup (no hardcoded secrets, no exposed ports that shouldn't be), 8) Dependencies are up to date and have no known vulnerabilities. Output: status (approved/needs_fixes), issues, approval to proceed to feature development.
steps.done_when: Sprint 0 is reviewed and approved, or issues are documented for fixing before proceeding.

steps.name: feature_backlog_initialization
steps.instruction: Initialize the executable feature backlog. Transform sprint_plans into a flat, ordered list of features to implement. Each entry: feature_id, feature_name, epic_id, sprint_id, user_story_ids (the stories this feature covers), depends_on_features (other feature_ids that must complete first), status (pending/in_progress/completed/blocked), template_ref ('feature_implementation_template' — the reusable step sequence defined below). Order the list respecting dependencies and sprint assignments. The orchestrator will iterate this list, and for each feature, instantiate the feature_implementation_template with that feature's context. Output: ordered feature list ready for sequential or parallel execution.
steps.done_when: feature_backlog exists as an ordered list of features, each referencing the implementation template and its specific context.

steps.name: sprint_boundary_check
steps.trigger: Execute after the last feature of each sprint completes
steps.instruction: Sprint boundary checkpoint. Execute when all features assigned to the current sprint are completed. Steps: 1) Summarize what was built this sprint (features completed, stories fulfilled), 2) Run full regression test suite on staging, 3) Compare actual velocity to planned velocity, 4) Identify any features that slipped or were cut, 5) Review next sprint's planned features against current state, 6) Check if any dependencies have changed, 7) Identify risks for next sprint. If velocity is significantly below plan (>30% slower), trigger needs_clarification presenting: what was completed, what slipped, options (extend timeline, cut scope, parallelize more), and ask human for direction. If on track, proceed to next sprint automatically. Output: sprint_id_completed, features_completed, velocity_ratio, next_sprint_adjustments, human_decision (if needed).
steps.done_when: Sprint is reviewed, next sprint is confirmed or adjusted, any human decisions are captured.

templates.name: Feature Implementation Cycle
templates.description: Reusable sequence for implementing any feature. The orchestrator instantiates this template for each feature in the feature_backlog, injecting the feature's specific context (user stories, design specs, API specs) as input_artifacts.

parameters.feature_id: ID of the feature being implemented
parameters.user_story_ids: List of user story IDs this feature implements

steps.name: feature_spec_review
steps.instruction: Review all relevant specifications for feature '{feature_name}'. Pull from context: 1) User stories matching {user_story_ids} from user_stories_refined — read every acceptance criterion, 2) Relevant screens from screen_specifications, 3) Relevant API endpoints from openapi_spec, 4) Relevant database tables from database_schema, 5) Relevant business rules from business_rules. Identify: gaps (anything undefined), ambiguities (anything interpretable multiple ways), conflicts (spec inconsistencies). If gaps or ambiguities are found and cannot be resolved from context, trigger needs_clarification. Output: feature_spec_summary (consolidated spec for this specific feature with all relevant details in one place), issues_found, clarifications_needed.

steps.name: feature_implementation_plan
steps.instruction: Create an ordered implementation plan for feature '{feature_name}' based on feature_spec_summary. Break into subtasks in this order: 1) db_migration (if schema changes needed — list tables/columns to add/modify), 2) backend_model (data models to create/update), 3) backend_service (business logic functions to implement), 4) backend_endpoints (API routes to create, with method and path), 5) backend_tests (unit tests for service layer, integration tests for endpoints), 6) frontend_types (TypeScript types to define), 7) frontend_state (state management additions), 8) frontend_api (API client functions), 9) frontend_components (new components or modifications to existing), 10) frontend_pages (page assembly and wiring), 11) frontend_states (loading, error, empty states), 12) frontend_responsive (responsive behavior), 13) frontend_a11y (accessibility additions), 14) frontend_tests (component and unit tests), 15) e2e_test (end-to-end test), 16) code_review, 17) visual_review, 18) staging_deploy. For each subtask: description of exactly what to implement, acceptance criteria, estimated effort. Skip any subtask type that doesn't apply to this feature.

steps.name: database_migration
steps.condition: Only execute if feature_impl_plan includes db_migration subtask
steps.instruction: Create the database migration for feature '{feature_name}' as specified in feature_impl_plan. Write the migration file using the project's migration tool. Include: CREATE TABLE or ALTER TABLE statements, new indexes, new constraints, new enum types. The migration must be reversible (include a down/rollback function). Run the migration locally, verify: new schema matches expectations, existing data is not corrupted, rollback works cleanly. Follow naming convention: timestamp_description.ts. Do not modify existing migration files — always create a new one.

steps.name: backend_model
steps.condition: Only execute if feature_impl_plan includes backend_model subtask
steps.instruction: Implement or update the data model/entity for feature '{feature_name}'. Create the model file in src/models/ or update existing. Define: all fields with types matching database_schema, validation rules matching business_rules (using zod or chosen validation library), type exports for use in services and routes, any computed fields or virtual properties. If using ORM: define relations, hooks (beforeCreate, beforeUpdate if needed), scopes/queries that will be commonly used. Follow existing patterns in the codebase. Export model type for use across the application.

steps.name: backend_service
steps.instruction: Implement the business logic service layer for feature '{feature_name}'. Create src/services/{feature_name}.service.ts. Implement functions for each operation: each function takes typed input, performs business logic, interacts with database via model layer, returns typed output. Enforce business_rules at this layer (not in the route handler). Handle errors by throwing typed AppError instances (from error handling setup). Keep functions pure where possible: receive dependencies as parameters, don't import global state. Implement: input validation (delegate to zod schemas), authorization checks (verify user has permission), data transformation (between API format and DB format), side effects (queue emails, emit events — but as function calls, not inline). Each function should have a clear single responsibility.

steps.name: backend_endpoints
steps.instruction: Implement API endpoint(s) for feature '{feature_name}' per openapi_spec. Create route file in src/routes/. For each endpoint: define route with method and path, attach middleware (auth guard if authentication_required, role check if role-restricted, rate limiter if specified, request validation with zod schema matching openapi_spec request body/query/params), handler function that: extracts validated data from request, calls service layer function, formats response per openapi_spec response schema, handles errors (service throws → error middleware catches). Follow REST conventions: 201 for create, 200 for read/update, 204 for delete, proper Location header on create. Register routes in the router.

steps.name: backend_middleware
steps.condition: Only execute if feature_impl_plan includes feature-specific middleware
steps.instruction: Implement any feature-specific middleware for '{feature_name}'.
